data.title: Federal Reserve's Barr warns about stablecoins; Trump says war will end in two to three weeks.
data.description:
Headline

▌Federal Reserve Governor Barr Warns of Stablecoin Risks, Emphasizes Need for Enhanced Regulation and Reserve Review

On April 1st, Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr stated that the development of stablecoins must be built on strict regulation, and warned that without effective constraints, it could repeat the "long history of problems caused by private currencies." In his speech, Barr pointed out that although the GENIUS Stablecoin Act has provided an initial regulatory framework for the industry, the key lies in subsequent implementation, including continuous monitoring of reserve assets and mechanisms to prevent illicit uses. He emphasized that stablecoins can only be truly considered "stable" if they can be repaid at face value under various market conditions. The liquidity and security of reserve assets are particularly critical when market pressures or the issuer's own risks intensify. Furthermore, Barr pointed out that stablecoin issuers have an incentive to increase profits through higher yields, which may lead them to take on higher risks in reserve asset management, thus posing a potential threat to financial stability.

Currently, the regulation of stablecoins remains a significant point of contention in US crypto legislation, and has to some extent affected the progress of related legislation. On April 1st, US President Trump stated that he expects the US to end the war against Iran within two to three weeks, implying that the US has essentially achieved its military objectives and leaving the resolution of the Strait of Hormuz issue to other countries. Trump told reporters at the White House on Tuesday, "I'd say within two weeks, maybe two weeks, maybe three weeks. We'll leave because we have no reason to continue doing this." Trump suggested that an agreement with the US could still be reached during this period. He said, "We're getting the job done, I think in about two weeks, maybe a few more days, but we're going to destroy everything they have. Now, it's possible we'll reach an agreement before then because we're going to hit bridges—we've already hit some, and we still have a few good bridges in mind. But if they're willing to sit down at the negotiating table, that would be a good thing." Trump also claimed that the US could end the war without an agreement with Iran. When we feel they are being forced back to the Stone Age and unable to produce nuclear weapons, we will leave, regardless of whether we reach an agreement. As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $68,213.78, a 24-hour change of +2.3%; ETH price is $2,100.56, a 24-hour change of +3.8%; BNB price is $616.50, a 24-hour change of +1.4%. SOL price is $82.94, up 0.6% in the last 24 hours; DOGE price is $0.09202, up 1.4% in the last 24 hours; XRP price is $1.34, up 1.1% in the last 24 hours; TRX price is $0.3132, down 1.9% in the last 24 hours. The WLFI price is $0.09881, a 24-hour change of +0.1%; the HYPE price is $36.40, a 24-hour change of -1.1%. Policy: The CFTC Enforcement Director stated on April 1st that combating insider trading, energy market manipulation, retail fraud, and anti-money laundering violations in prediction markets are priorities.

▌Dubai Releases Regulatory Framework for Crypto Derivatives

On March 31, the Dubai Virtual Asset Regulatory Authority (VARA) released version 2.1 of its "Trading Platform Service Rules Manual," clarifying the methods for licensed crypto companies to provide crypto derivatives trading in Dubai. The framework covers requirements such as customer suitability, leverage and margin control, asset segregation, information disclosure standards, and regulatory intervention authority. This framework applies to licensed virtual asset service providers (VASPs) operating in Dubai.

▌PBOC: Continue to Implement Moderately Loose Monetary Policy, Increase Counter-cyclical and Cross-cyclical Adjustments

On March 31, the People's Bank of China's Monetary Policy Committee held its first quarterly meeting (the 112th overall) of 2026 on March 26.

The meeting analyzed the domestic and international economic and financial situation, concluding that the impact of changes in the external environment is deepening, the global economic momentum is weak, geopolitical and trade conflicts are frequent, the economic performance of major economies is diverging, and there is uncertainty regarding inflation trends and monetary policy adjustments. my country's economy is generally stable and progressing steadily, with new achievements in high-quality development, but it still faces problems and challenges such as strong supply and weak demand, and external shocks. It is necessary to continue implementing a moderately loose monetary policy, increase counter-cyclical and cross-cyclical adjustments, better leverage the dual functions of monetary policy tools in terms of both quantity and structure, strengthen the coordination of monetary and fiscal policies, and promote stable economic growth and a reasonable recovery in prices.

▌Iranian President: To End the War, the Other Side Must Guarantee No More Aggression

On April 1, Iranian President Pezechzian stated on March 31 that Iran has the "necessary will" to end the war, provided that the other side meets Iran's demands, especially providing a necessary guarantee that it will no longer aggress.

According to Iran's Nol News Agency, Pezehitzian, in a phone call with European Council President Costa on the same day, stated that the solution to normalization is to stop the aggressive attacks by the United States and Israel. He reiterated that Iran has not sought tension and war at any stage and "possesses the necessary will to end this war." Pezehitzian pointed out that Iran entered into negotiations with the United States with sincerity and a constructive attitude, but was illegally attacked midway through the negotiations. This issue proves that the United States does not believe in diplomacy, but is merely "seeking to issue orders to achieve its greedy ambitions." (Xinhua News Agency)

▌US Official: Third-Party Mediation Ineffective, Direct Negotiations with Iran Should Be Conducted

On April 1, a US official expressed concern about conducting indirect negotiations with Iran through third parties, believing that direct negotiations would be more efficient. The official stated that although Pakistan is willing to assist in mediating the conflict, the US has reservations about using Islamabad as a negotiation venue, mainly due to security risks. In addition, the official also pointed out that previous mediation through Oman had not been effective.

This marks the company's largest funding round to date, supporting its expensive investments in chips, data centers, and talent. The majority of the funds in this months-long funding round came from three major technology companies. Amazon agreed to invest $50 billion in this round, while Nvidia and SoftBank Group each invested $30 billion. A significant portion of Amazon's investment ($35 billion) hinges on OpenAI's ability to go public or achieve technological milestones in general artificial intelligence. This is the first time OpenAI has raised over $3 billion from individual investors through banking channels. OpenAI stated that the company currently generates $2 billion in revenue per month. Enterprise sales currently account for 40% of its revenue, and this proportion is expected to increase to 50% by the end of the year.

▌Cross-border remittance startup OpenFX raises $94 million, led by Pantera and others

On March 31, OpenFX, a forex market-making and cross-border remittance startup, raised $94 million. The round was led by Accel, Lightspeed Faction, M13, Northzone, and Pantera, valuing the company at approximately $500 million post-money. OpenFX provides faster and lower-cost settlement services for large cross-border funds by bridging the banking system and blockchain infrastructure with stablecoins. OpenFX plans to use the funds to expand into Southeast Asia and Latin America.

... Cryptocurrency

Google: Future Quantum Computers Could Crack Bitcoin Private Keys in Just 9 Minutes

On March 31, Google research showed that future quantum computers could crack Bitcoin private keys in just 9 minutes, one minute faster than the average Bitcoin block time. Mempool attacks now pose a real threat, urging us to immediately migrate to post-quantum cryptographic schemes.

▌Bitmine adds 167,578 ETH to its stake, worth approximately $342.4 million

On March 31, according to OnchainLens, a blockchain analyst, Bitmine added 167,578 ETH to its stake, worth approximately $342.4 million. Its total staked ETH has now reached 3,310,221 ETH, with a total value of approximately $6.7 billion.

▌MetaPlanet receives $255 million in funding, potentially to purchase over 3,800 BTC

On March 31, MetaPlanet received $255 million in funding from previously announced global institutional investors.

This sum of money could purchase over 3,800 BTC at the current price, potentially making it the third-largest BTC holder globally. According to Jinse Finance, on March 31st, Bitcoin treasury company Nakamoto released its 2025 annual report on March 30th, disclosing that it sold approximately 284 BTC in March 2026, realizing approximately $20 million (an average selling price of approximately $70,422), to establish a dedicated dollar operating reserve for short-term liquidity needs such as paying for strategic integration activities and Kraken loan interest. The financial report shows that the company's weighted average cost of Bitcoin holdings was $118,171 per coin, while the market price of Bitcoin as of December 31, 2025, was only $87,519. The company recorded a loss of $166.2 million for the year due to changes in the fair value of digital assets. At the end of the year, the company held a total of 5,342 Bitcoins. The company stated that it still considers Bitcoin a long-term strategic reserve asset, and this sale is merely for short-term liquidity management, not a strategic shift.

▌A whale withdrew 1,635 BTC from CEXs in the past week, equivalent to approximately $110.7 million

On March 31, according to Lookonchain monitoring, the whale wallet 15oD9P withdrew 1,635 BTC ($110.7 million) from Bybit, Binance, Bullish.com, and OKX in the past week.

**Circle Minted Approximately 750 Million USDC on Solana in the Past 24 Hours** According to SolanaFloor monitoring on March 31st, Circle minted approximately 750 million USDC on Solana in the past 24 hours. **Coinbase to List EDGEX (EDGEX)** Coinbase announced that it will list EDGEX (EDGEX). The EDGEX-USD trading pair will open later today, provided liquidity conditions are met and trading is supported.

▌SBF Questions the Impartiality of the Southern District of New York Judge, Calls for His Withdrawal from Cases

On March 31, FTX founder SBF posted that the Chief Judge of Delaware had recused himself from three cases due to alleged bias, while Judge Kaplan, the U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York, continued to hear his cases. SBF accused Judge Kaplan of showing contempt to the jury, providing pizza and transportation to facilitate a quick verdict, and making comments about fraudulent evidence before the sentencing. SBF called on Judge Kaplan to recuse himself from his cases.

... **Important Economic Developments** **Buffett: Stock Market Valuations Unattractive, Still Deeply Involved in Investment Decisions** March 31st - In an interview with CNBC, Warren Buffett, the "Oracle of Omaha," stated that stock market valuations remain unattractive, and Berkshire Hathaway has not found many buyable targets in the market after this year's stock market decline. Buffett downplayed recent market volatility, saying the current environment is far from creating significant buying opportunities in the past: "Since I took over, the market has fallen by more than 50% at least three times. There's nothing to be excited about right now." Even after stepping down as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, the 95-year-old Buffett remains deeply involved in investment decisions. He says he still goes to the office every day to continuously monitor the market. His routine includes calling Mark Millard, head of Berkshire's financial assets, before the market opens to discuss market dynamics. Millard will execute deals based on these discussions, indicating that although Buffett handed over the CEO position to Abel in early 2026, he remains personally involved.

▌US Defense Secretary: The Next Few Days Will Be the Decisive Moment in the War with Iran

March 31, US Defense Secretary Hergsays: The next few days will be the decisive moment in the war with Iran, and Iran is powerless to do anything about it. Iran has undergone regime change. If Iran is wise, it will reach an agreement. If Iran does not reach an agreement, the war will continue in a more intense manner. US President Trump is willing to reach an agreement.

▌Trump Says the War with Iran Will End Soon, Strait of Hormuz Will "Open Automatically"

March 31, according to the New York Post, Trump said in an interview on Tuesday that he believes the war with Iran may end soon, and other countries can reopen the Strait of Hormuz on their own.

“We’re not going to be there for long. We’re completely destroying them right now,” Trump said in a phone interview. Trump claimed that the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran had closed for 31 days, had caused global energy prices to soar, and that other countries could resolve the issue on their own. “I think it will automatically reopen, but my attitude is that I’ve destroyed this country, and let the countries that use the strait open it themselves…because I think the people who control the oil will be very happy to reopen the strait.” When pressed about a Wall Street Journal report that he was willing to end the war without reopening the strait, Trump said, “Honestly, I’m not thinking about that. My only job is to make sure they don’t have nuclear weapons. They don’t have nuclear weapons. The strait will automatically open when we leave.” ▌Iranian Foreign Minister: No Negotiations with the US, But Information Exchanged On April 1, local time March 31, Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi stated that the current situation was “not about negotiations,” but rather about information exchanged through direct channels or with “regional friends.” Iran continues to receive messages from U.S. representative Witkov, but this does not signify the commencement of negotiations; the exchanges primarily consist of "warnings or exchanges of views." He pointed out that Iran is not currently negotiating with any specific party, and the information exchange is being conducted by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs under the coordination of security agencies and strictly within the official framework, under the supervision of the government and the Supreme National Security Council. Araghchi emphasized that Iran has not responded to any of the 15 proposals put forward by the United States, nor has it made any proposals or conditions. Iran has not yet made a final decision on the principles of the negotiations, but the conditions for ending the war are "very clear": Iran "will not agree to a ceasefire," but demands a "complete end to the war throughout the region." A Reuters/Ipsos poll on April 1 found that two-thirds of Americans believe the U.S. should end its involvement in the war against Iran as soon as possible, even if it means failing to achieve the goals set by the Trump administration. In a survey conducted from Friday to Sunday last week, approximately 66% of respondents expressed this view, while 27% said the U.S. should strive to achieve all its objectives in Iran, even if the conflict is protracted. 6% of respondents did not answer the question. Among Trump's Republicans, 40% supported ending the conflict as soon as possible, even if U.S. objectives cannot be achieved, while 57% supported a long-term intervention. In this survey of 1,021 people, 60% of respondents disapproved of a U.S. military strike against Iran, while 35% approved. Two-thirds of respondents said they expected gasoline prices to worsen further over the next year, with Republicans accounting for 40% of that number. Trump: Powell must leave the Fed chairmanship as soon as possible. On April 1, U.S. President Trump stated that Powell must leave the Fed chairmanship as soon as possible.

▌The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in April is 98.4%

On April 1st, according to CME's "FedWatch": the probability of the Fed raising interest rates by 25 basis points in April is 1.6%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 98.4%. The probability of the Fed cumulatively cutting interest rates by 25 basis points by June is 3.9%, the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 94.6%, and the probability of cumulatively raising interest rates by 25 basis points is 1.5%.

Golden Encyclopedia

▌Can Bitcoin still be called "digital gold" under geopolitical crises?

Every time there is a geopolitical crisis, gold prices rise, and Bitcoin prices plummet. After six tests, the claim of "digital gold" has never been confirmed by data. Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves.

For investors, Bitcoin exhibits an asymmetry: it falls with stocks but doesn't rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an overabundance of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation). Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-useful asset," capable of functioning when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries narrow, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold, but rather a completely new "next-generation gold." Generational shifts and the widespread adoption of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.

data.title: Of the 35 trillion stablecoins, only 1% has actually been spent.
data.description: In 1973, banks from 15 countries sat in a conference room in Brussels and decided to replace the chaotic cross-border telex with a standardized telegraphic protocol. This system later became known as SWIFT. The significance of this event never lay in the technological sophistication, but in the simple fact that whoever controls the settlement channels can extract a cut from every global money flow. For fifty years, there were no traffic lights or alternative routes. Until someone decided to abandon this path. By 2025, the total amount of stablecoin settlements on the blockchain will reach $35 trillion. This figure is larger than Mastercard's annual processing volume. But a joint report by McKinsey and Artemis Analytics reveals an embarrassing truth: only about $380 billion, or 1% of the total, is actually used for payments. Stablecoins haven't revolutionized payments yet. But they are redefining what the word "settlement" really means. Where did 99% of the money go? $35 trillion sounds like stablecoins have already eaten up a huge chunk of the global payments pie. But if you break down that number, the picture is completely different. Visa's 2025 on-chain analytics report directly called these transactions "noise"—arbitrage bots moving money between different DEXs, leaving millions of dollars' worth of traces on the chain with a single strategy, but without any real goods or services being exchanged; exchanges moving money back and forth between cold and hot wallets, with billions of dollars of USDT moving between different chains during periods of market volatility, purely for internal accounting. Moving money from one pocket to another and then claiming to have created two transactions—this is how a large part of the prosperity of the on-chain world comes about. The industry invented a metric called "adjusted transaction volume" to specifically filter out bots, internal transfers, and duplicate calculations. After filtering, 35 trillion shrinks to about 9 to 10 trillion US dollars. Furthermore, actual payments—where someone pays and someone receives—are only 380 to 390 billion, roughly 4% of the adjusted transaction volume. Sounds like stablecoins are exaggerating? Not entirely. This 4% year-over-year growth rate is close to 100%. The problem isn't the small number, but that the vast majority of on-chain activity is essentially machines talking to machines. The real spenders: Not you and me, but businesses. Of that 1% of real payments, over 60% come from businesses. McKinsey data shows that B2B stablecoin payments will reach $261 billion in 2025, a year-on-year increase of over 6 times. This isn't speculation; it's real money. This isn't an isolated phenomenon. Cross-border supply chain settlements reached $130 billion, global salary and remittance payments were approximately $90 billion (according to McKinsey's Global Payments Map tracking data, this represents less than 1% of the over $100 trillion total transaction volume in this sector), and even the procurement of AI computing power and cloud resources is starting to be settled using stablecoins, amounting to approximately $11 billion. Stablecoins have undergone a transformation in the B2B market: from speculative tokens on exchanges to productivity tools for corporate finance departments. For companies that have been burdened with SWIFT tolls for years, this isn't some "Web3 vision," it's simply about saving money. Why is no one using it on the consumer side? Having discussed the rapid growth in the B2B market, the data for the C2C market is remarkably bleak. Even combining B2B and C2C, all stablecoin payments account for only about 0.02% of the total global annual payments, and 60% of that is paid by businesses. The share left for ordinary consumers is statistically negligible. The reason isn't that complicated. In markets like the US, stablecoins aren't legal tender. Using them to buy a cup of coffee is legally equivalent to asset disposal. At the end of the year, you have to precisely record the difference between the cost price and market price of those few dollars to calculate capital gains tax. For a $5 cup of coffee, the compliance costs might be higher than the coffee itself. Using Visa? Zero tax burden, plus cashback. Merchants aren't ready either. Walmart and Amazon don't accept direct stablecoin payments. For these retail giants, integrating blockchain payment gateways means entirely new accounting, anti-money laundering monitoring, and volatility risk management. The returns are unclear, and confirmation is needed. But a workaround is quietly taking effect. Visa and Mastercard have launched stablecoin-linked cards. Users hold stablecoins in their digital wallets; when the card is swiped, the gateway automatically converts the stablecoins into local fiat currency, while the merchant receives US dollars or euros. In 2025, spending on these cards grew by 673%, reaching $4.5 billion. Users don't need to understand gas fees or know they're using blockchain. Perhaps this is the true way stablecoins break into the mainstream: not by persuading people to change their payment habits, but by making them completely unaware of the change. An efficiency tool in developed countries, a lifeline for developing countries. The global landscape of stablecoins is extremely uneven, revealing a deeper truth. Asia accounts for 60% of global stablecoin payments, approximately $245 billion. The Monetary Authority of Singapore and the Hong Kong Monetary Authority have established licensing systems for stablecoin issuers, and Japan has followed suit. In these places, stablecoins are efficiency tools; businesses use them to replace cumbersome correspondent banking networks for faster and cheaper cross-border settlements. Everything operates within a compliant framework—clean, transparent, and traceable. Then shift your focus south. In Argentina, inflation is in the triple digits. In Nigeria, you have to go to the black market to buy US dollars. In Brazil, a Chainalysis report shows that over 90% of cryptocurrency flows are related to stablecoins, primarily used not for trading or speculation, but for safe-haven savings and remittances back home. But looking at McKinsey's "official payments" statistics, Africa and Latin America combined account for less than $1 billion. Where did the data go? The answer is that a large portion of payments in these regions occur on over-the-counter platforms and P2P markets, completely undetectable by compliant payment interfaces. In developed markets, stablecoins are tools for faster settlements. In developing markets, stablecoins are the only "digital dollar" you can encounter. One is about efficiency upgrades, the other about survival needs. The same technology, two completely different stories. From a technical perspective, low-fee public blockchains, whether Layer 2 solutions or high-performance chains, are making it possible to complete cross-border transfers for a few cents. The implementation of account abstraction technology has further removed the final hurdle: users no longer need to hold native tokens to pay gas fees; merchants can pay on their behalf, or even directly use stablecoins to pay fees. Blockchain wallets are now virtually indistinguishable from Alipay. In summary, the "1%" in the McKinsey report is not the epitaph of stablecoins, but their benchmark. This 1% precisely targets the most painful and inefficient part of the global financial system: cross-border B2B settlements and the survival finance of emerging markets. The US GENIUS Act, which will take effect in July 2025, the EU's MiCA, and Hong Kong's stablecoin regulations are paving a legal highway for this 1%. Perhaps one day, maybe in 2026, a Brazilian will transfer $50 to his family via WhatsApp. He won't know that the money is going through the blockchain, nor does he need to. The best infrastructure is one where you don't even feel its presence.
